title: 10 best herbal creams for sunburn

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with sunburn because they contain a powerful soothing agent that quickly calms red, inflamed skin. The gel-like substance in aloe vera has natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by sunburn. Additionally, its hydrating properties help to replenish lost moisture and promote skin regeneration, speeding up the healing process and reducing the appearance of peeling and flaking skin. This makes aloe creams an effective remedy for providing instant relief from sunburned skin.
remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Aloe vera gel contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Hydrating and Moisturizing Properties: Aloe vera's high water content (up to 95%) helps to lock in moisture, soothing dry, itchy skin and reducing the appearance of peeling and flakiness caused by sunburn.

Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidants present in aloe vera, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of long-term skin damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain a gel-like substance that helps soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Roots: Aloe roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and extract their gel using a juicer or blender.
Combine the extracted aloe vera gel with coconut oil at a ratio of 3:2 and stir well.
Add chamomile essential oil at a dosage of 10 drops per ounce of mixture and blend thoroughly.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 24 hours in a cool dark place before whipping it into a creamy consistency.
Transfer the cream to sterilized containers and refrigerate immediately to prolong shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with sunburn because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, including flavonoids and carotenoids, work to reduce redness and swelling, while also promoting collagen production to aid in the healing process. Additionally, pot marigold's natural antimicrobial properties help prevent infection and promote a healthy recovery from sunburn. As a result, using pot marigold creams can provide fast and effective relief for sunburned skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold cream contains flavonoids, such as luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream, including kaempferol and quercetin, help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals and promote healing after a sunburn.

Soothing: Pot marigold has been traditionally used to soothe skin irritations and wounds, and its cream can provide relief from the discomfort and itching associated with sunburn, while also promoting skin hydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm skin irritations caused by sunburn.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids and carotenoids, flowers provide natural protection against UV radiation and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh calendula officinalis petals and dry them for 2 weeks in a warm dark place.
Infuse the dried calendula petals in 100ml of high-quality carrier oil such as sweet almond or olive oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids then mix with 50g of beeswax.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax is melted and fully incorporated taking about 30 minutes to an hour.
Pour the cooled mixture into a clean sterilized glass jar and label it for future use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb's apigenin content calms redness, swelling, and itching associated with sunburn, while its antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells. Additionally, chamomile's emollient nature moisturizes and cools the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing. As a result, applying herbal chamomile creams to affected areas helps alleviate the painful and itchy symptoms of sunburn, providing quick relief and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile cream helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with sunburn, soothing the skin and promoting healing.
Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals generated by UV radiation, preventing premature aging and skin cell death.

Soothing and calming: Chamomile's apigenin content has a direct effect on the skin's receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing stress-induced redness and irritation, making it an excellent topical treatment for sunburn relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in azulene, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Roots: Chamomile roots contain apigenin, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes healing of damaged skin caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 20 grams of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers for optimal potency retention.
Combine the dried flowers with 50 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 4 weeks allowing the active compounds to infuse and extract slowly.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids completely.
Mix 100 milliliters of the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of coconut oil melting them together in a double boiler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with sunburn because of its natural cooling and soothing properties. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sunburn. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects help to hydrate dry skin, promoting a faster recovery from sun damage. As a result, English lavender creams can provide quick relief and comfort to sunburned skin, reducing discomfort and promoting a healthy-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburn, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing burned skin.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender contains antioxidants that help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV rays, reducing the risk of prolonged healing time and potential scarring.
Calming and anti-irritating properties: The calming effects of lavender oil can help ease itching and discomfort associated with sunburn, promoting a sense of relaxation and relief for affected skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots contain compounds like linalool and camphor, which help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Flowers: Lavender flowers have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in quantities of 2 cups.
Combine harvested flowers with distilled water at a ratio of 1:4 and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Mix 1/2 cup coconut oil, 1/4 cup beeswax, and 1 tablespoon vitamin e oil into the liquid extract.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 20 minutes until emulsified.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with sunburn because they contain soothing, anti-inflammatory ingredients that calm irritated skin. The aloe vera and vitamin E in these creams work to reduce redness and swelling, while the marshmallow root provides a protective barrier that locks in moisture and promotes healing. This combination of properties helps to alleviate discomfort, dryness, and itching associated with sunburn, leaving skin feeling soft, smooth, and comfortable once again.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow extracts contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Soil-thickening and moisturizing: The mucilage in marshmallows creates a protective barrier on the skin that soothes and locks in moisture, providing relief from dryness and irritation caused by sun exposure.
Antioxidant: Marshmallow extracts contain antioxidants like vitamin E, which help neutralize free radicals formed during sunburn, reducing oxidative stress and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Barks: The barks of the marshmallow plant contain mucilages that soothe and calm sunburned skin, providing a protective barrier against further irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 50 grams of fresh marshmallow root for future use in the cream recipe.
Combine 200 milliliters of distilled water and 20 grams of dried marshmallow root in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to extract the active compounds from the marshmallow root.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids then add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with sunburn because they contain natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the skin. The plantain extract has been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sunburned skin. Additionally, its emollient properties help lock in moisture, reducing peeling and flaking of the skin. As a result, plantain creams provide instant relief from sunburn discomfort, allowing for faster recovery and improved skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain contains anti-inflammatory agents like allantoin, which helps to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn by reducing inflammation in the skin.

Antioxidants: Plantain's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals generated during sun exposure, reducing the risk of long-term skin damage and promoting faster healing.
Skin-soothing glycosides: Plantain contains glycosides that have a cooling effect on the skin, helping to calm and soothe sunburned skin, reduce itching and discomfort, and promote skin hydration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn, while also soothing irritated skin.
Barks: Plantain barks are rich in antioxidants that neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried plantago major leaves from trusted sources ensure freshness and potency.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle process takes about 10 minutes.
Combine 20g of the powdered plantago major with 80g of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan stir until well combined preparation time is approximately 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 1 hour, stirring occasionally, to facilitate absorption and infusion of herbal properties temperature should not exceed 70 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids and allowing the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature takes about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with sunburn because they contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, the creams' moisturizing properties help to hydrate the skin, promoting a healthy barrier function that protects against further irritation. As a result, herbal red clover creams provide relief from sunburned skin, allowing it to heal faster and more effectively.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Red clover contains anti-inflammatory compounds like isoflavones, which help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn by minimizing inflammation.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in red clover cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting faster healing.

Vasoactive compounds: Red clover's vasoactive compounds, including isoflavones and saponins, help improve blood flow to the affected area, which promotes healing, reduces redness, and soothes discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them ideal for reducing sunburn discomfort.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of omega-3 fatty acids, they help to moisturize and nourish the skin, while also providing antioxidant protection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried red clover flowers trifolium pratense and store them in an airtight container.
Combine the dried red clover flowers with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 65°c for 30 minutes to infuse the herbs into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids after 15 minutes of pressing.
Mix the infused oil with 200g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil, then heat at 75°c for 10 minutes to create a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with sunburn because of their unique ability to soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that work together to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. As the cream is applied directly to the affected area, it helps to restore the skin's natural barrier function, promoting faster healing and reducing the appearance of blisters. This natural remedy provides relief from the discomfort and distress caused by sunburn, allowing you to feel more comfortable and confident in your own skin.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with sunburn by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, providing relief from discomfort.
Hypericin: Has antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and skin damage.
Flavonoids: Act as a natural anti-histamine, blocking the release of histamine and reducing allergic reactions, itching, and inflammation associated with sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort contain hyperforin, a natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe sunburned skin.
Flowers: The flowers of St. John's Wort are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to possess photoprotective properties, helping to shield the skin from further UV damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves of st. john's wort in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 60°f for 24 hours or air-drying on paper towels for several weeks.
Grind 2 cups dried hypericum perforatum flowers into a fine powder and mix with 1 cup beeswax pellets, 1/4 cup coconut oil, and 1/4 cup sweet almond oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove from heat and stir until cooled to around 100°f before transferring the cream to sterilized glass jars with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with sunburn because of its unique properties. The antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil, such as terpinen-4-ol and caryophyllene oxide, work together to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with sunburn. Additionally, the astringent and antiseptic properties of tea tree oil help to cool and refresh the skin, providing instant relief from discomfort and itching caused by sun exposure.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Help to prevent infection and reduce inflammation by killing bacteria, viruses, and fungi on the skin's surface.
Aloe Vera's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Soothe and calm sunburned skin by reducing redness, swelling, and pain caused by inflammation.
Green Tea's Antioxidant Properties: Neutralize free radicals that can cause further damage to the skin after sun exposure, promoting faster healing and reducing the appearance of sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea tree plant contain compounds like terpinen-4-ol, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing and relieving sunburned skin.
Leaves: Tea tree leaves are rich in antioxidants and essential oils that help to calm and reduce redness associated with sunburn, while also promoting wound healing and tissue repair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or air circulation techniques over several weeks to preserve its essential oils.
Combine 20% of dried melaleuca alternifolia plant material with 80% distilled water in a heat-resistant mixing bowl, allowing it to steep for at least two hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused liquid for cream preparation.

Mix one part of the infused melaleuca alternifolia liquid with three parts of cold-pressed coconut oil and one part of beeswax, melting them in a double boiler over medium heat for approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with sunburn because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the skin. The cooling properties of ginger also help to reduce heat and discomfort associated with sunburn. Additionally, herbal extracts in the cream work to repair damaged skin cells and promote collagen production, which can aid in reducing redness and promoting faster healing. As a result, ginger creams provide quick relief from sunburned skin, leaving it feeling calm, cool, and comforted.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, which helps prevent further damage to the skin and reduces the risk of long-term skin problems like premature aging and skin cancer.

Analgesic properties: The analgesic compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and sesquiterpenes, help relieve itching, stinging, and discomfort associated with sunburn, providing fast and effective pain relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginger roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant, also known as rhyzomes, contain antioxidants and analgesics that reduce redness and pain caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root weighing about 200 grams rinse it gently under running water.
Grate the ginger root using a box grater into small pieces measuring approximately 10 milliliters.
Combine grated ginger with 250 milliliters of coconut oil in a stainless steel mixing bowl heating it at low temperature for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter squeezing out excess liquid allowing it to cool down completely within two hours.
Mix cooled ginger infusion with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of aloe vera gel whipping it until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The licorice extract contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which calms redness and swelling caused by sun exposure. Additionally, licorice has been shown to have antioxidant properties, helping to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate sun damage. When applied topically, herbal licorice creams provide instant relief from sunburned skin, reducing discomfort and promoting a faster recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice extract has been shown to reduce inflammation caused by sunburn, soothing redness and swelling, and helping to relieve discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Licorice contains antioxidants like glycyrrhizin, which help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals generated during UV radiation, promoting healthy skin recovery.

Soothing and calming effects: The saponins present in licorice extract have a calming effect on the skin, reducing itching, irritation, and discomfort associated with sunburn, while also helping to restore the skin's natural barrier function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming sunburned skin.
Barks: Contain glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and irritation caused by sunburn, promoting faster healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder from a reputable supplier for future use.
Combine 100 milliliters of distilled water and 10 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it is fully incorporated at a temperature of 70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Mix in 20 milliliters of glycerin and 2 grams of vitamin e oil with a spatula for an additional 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool before refrigerating for at least 24 hours for proper solidification.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort creams helps with sunburn because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the skin. The cream's active ingredients, such as centella asiatica, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort creams contain moisturizing agents that hydrate the skin, reducing peeling and discomfort. This natural formula provides rapid relief from sunburn pain, itchiness, and dryness, allowing the skin to recover quickly and return to its normal state.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Asiatic pennywort contains compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant: The cream's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting faster recovery.

Wound healing: Asiatic pennywort has been shown to accelerate wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and reducing scar formation, making it an effective treatment for sunburn-related skin damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Rhyzomes: Contain potent natural antibacterial agents that prevent infection and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest centella asiatica leaves and flowers at dawn, ensuring 100g of fresh material for subsequent processing.
Blend the harvested centella asiatica material with distilled water in a ratio of 1:10 and leave it to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and combine the extracted liquid with 20% glycerin, 15% beeswax, and 5% vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes under stirring until the ingredients are fully incorporated and a smooth consistency is achieved.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken before filling it into sterilized jars, with a shelf life of six months when stored in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with sunburn because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the skin. The natural antioxidants present in sage work to reduce redness and swelling caused by UV radiation, promoting a quicker recovery from sun damage. Additionally, sage's emollient qualities moisturize and hydrate the skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort associated with sunburned areas. As a result, using herbal sage creams can provide fast relief and promote healthy-looking skin even after exposure to the sun.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, preventing further damage to the skin and promoting healing.
Wound-healing agents: Sage cream contains compounds that stimulate collagen production and improve wound healing, which can aid in the recovery process from sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain flavonoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in sun-damaged skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves and dry them for at least 24 hours in a well-ventilated area.
Combine 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and incorporated.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove any solids and filter it again if necessary.
Pour the final product into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The active compound curcumin in turmeric cream reduces inflammation and redness caused by UV exposure, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of peeling. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect damaged skin cells from further harm, leaving sunburned skin looking healthier and more radiant with continued use.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation caused by sunburn, leading to faster healing and reduced pain.
Antioxidant: Curcumin and other antioxidants in turmeric creams neutralize free radicals produced by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting natural recovery.
Wound-healing: Turmeric's antimicrobial and antiseptic properties help prevent infection, promote collagen synthesis, and accelerate wound healing, reducing the appearance of sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it an effective ingredient in soothing and protecting against sunburn.
Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are rich in volatile oils that have natural analgesic and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce pain, redness, and inflammation caused by sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including curcumin powder, coconut oil, beeswax, and aloe vera gel in equal proportions.
Heat coconut oil in a double boiler or a heatproof bowl set over simmering water for 10-15 minutes.
Add beeswax to the melted coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated, taking approximately 5 minutes.
Stir in curcumin powder and aloe vera gel mixture into the heated oil-wax mixture, allowing it to infuse for 20-30 minutes.
Remove from heat, let it cool slightly, then whip with an electric mixer or whisk until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with sunburn because they contain natural ingredients that soothe and calm the skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of elderberry, a key ingredient in many herbal elder creams, reduce redness and swelling caused by sun exposure. Additionally, the moisturizing effects of the cream help to hydrate parched skin, reducing peeling and flakiness associated with sunburn. As a result, herbal elder creams provide rapid relief from discomfort and promote healthy skin regeneration, allowing users to recover quickly from a sunburn and enjoy the great outdoors again.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder cream contains flavonoids, anthocyanins, and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease redness associated with sunburn, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming the skin.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in elder cream, such as vitamin C, beta-carotene, and polyphenols, help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, which can damage skin cells and accelerate aging. By neutralizing these free radicals, elder cream helps protect the skin from further damage.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in elder cream, such as triterpenes and flavonoids, help combat bacterial infections that can occur when the skin is damaged by sunburn, reducing the risk of infection and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming sunburned skin.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes contain flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce inflammation and promote the healing process of sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers at dawn and wash them thoroughly with cold water to remove any impurities.
Dry the washed flowers in a warm oven set to 150°f for 12 hours or until crispy to preserve their potency.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried sambucus nigra flowers with 1 cup of distilled water in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 8 hours.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into another container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 1/2 cup of heavy cream and store the resulting sambucus nigra cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers creams helps with sunburn because they contain natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb cleavers has been used for centuries to treat skin conditions, and its inclusion in creams provides rapid relief from redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by sun exposure. The cream's cooling effect also helps to reduce heat and itching, allowing the skin to heal faster and minimizing the risk of peeling or blistering.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cleavers has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and calm irritated skin, potentially reducing redness and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant: The herb is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by UV radiation, which may contribute to accelerated aging and sun-related issues.

Wound-healing properties: Cleavers has been traditionally used to treat wounds and skin ulcers due to its ability to promote tissue repair and regeneration. This property may also be beneficial for accelerating the healing process in cases of mild sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which soothe and calm skin irritations caused by sunburn.
Rhizomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and stems for approximately one hour in early morning.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for six hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried galium aparine with 50 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over medium heat, stirring occasionally, for approximately one hour until melted and smooth.
Pour the creamy mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow to cool at room temperature for four hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with sunburn because they contain allantoin, a natural ingredient that accelerates skin regeneration and promotes healing. Allantoin works by stimulating collagen production, reducing inflammation, and soothing itchy skin. Additionally, comfrey's rich content of vitamins A, C, and E provides antioxidant protection, helping to repair damaged skin cells and prevent further damage from UV radiation. As a result, comfrey creams can provide quick relief for sunburned skin, reducing redness, swelling, and discomfort while promoting a faster recovery time.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey's allantoin content helps to accelerate the skin's natural regeneration process, promoting faster healing and reducing the appearance of sunburned skin.

Vitamins A and E: The high levels of vitamins A and E in comfrey cream provide antioxidant protection, helping to neutralize free radicals caused by UV damage and reducing inflammation and redness associated with sunburn.
Alantolactone: Comfrey's alantolactone compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with severe sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain allantoin, a natural compound that helps accelerate skin regeneration and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried symphytum officinale herb, witch hazel, and sweet almond oil for infusion.
Combine one part dried symphytum officinale herb with two parts boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to extract the medicinal properties of the symphytum officinale herb.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids and measuring out one part infused liquid with three parts sweet almond oil.

Mix the infused liquid and sweet almond oil blend with 10% witch hazel solution in a double boiler, stirring constantly until the mixture reaches a comfortable consistency for application within 30 minutes of preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with sunburn because of its unique composition, which combines the anti-inflammatory properties of burdock root extract with the moisturizing benefits of natural oils. The extract soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with sunburn. Meanwhile, the cream's emollients lock in moisture to hydrate parched skin, promoting faster healing and minimizing peeling. As a result, burdock creams provide effective relief from sunburn discomfort, helping to restore healthy-looking skin in no time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Burdock cream's anti-inflammatory compound, lignan, helps reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn by inhibiting prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of burdock cream help neutralize free radicals generated by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting healing.

Soothing and protective property: Burdock's mucilages and polysaccharides create a soothing film on the skin that helps to calm and protect it from irritation, reducing discomfort and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and antioxidants that moisturize and protect the skin from further damage caused by sun exposure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest arctium lappa roots and dry them for at least 14 days to ensure potency and stability.
Grind 30g of dried arctium lappa root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or blender.
Mix the powdered arctium lappa with 100g of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 65°c to 70°c.
Remove from heat, let cool slightly, then pour into sterilized glass containers and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with sunburn because they possess potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin. The cream's natural antioxidants and flavonoids work to neutralize free radicals, reducing redness and swelling associated with sunburned skin. Additionally, dandelion's aloe-like properties provide long-lasting hydration, helping to replenish moisture lost due to sun exposure. This combination of benefits allows for rapid relief from sunburn discomfort, promoting faster healing and preventing further damage. As a result, herbal dandelion creams offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating the symptoms of sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage.

Skin soothing and moisturizing properties: Dandelion cream's fatty acid content, including linoleic acid and oleic acid, can help soothe and moisturize sunburned skin, reducing discomfort and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and soothing sun-damaged skin.
Seeds: Containing high levels of fatty acids and omega-3 oils, they help moisturize and repair damaged skin, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves according to local regulations.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until crispy.
Combine 200g dried dandelion flowers and leaves with 400ml sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture for 4 hours stirring occasionally or until the oil reaches 55°c to 60°c.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and store in the refrigerator.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for sunburn are natural topical products infused with potent botanicals, such as aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula, that provide soothing relief from sun-damaged skin. These creams help to calm redness, reduce inflammation, and promote healing by gently moisturizing the skin while also offering antioxidant protection. For example, creams containing aloe vera and lavender oil can effectively ease discomfort and promote faster recovery from sunburn. This means individuals can enjoy outdoor activities without worrying about painful and itchy skin, allowing them to fully appreciate nature's beauty while protecting their skin health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat sunburn with herbal creams is to apply topical remedies rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. Aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula creams provide soothing relief from redness, itching, and pain. Additionally, cooling herbs like peppermint and eucalyptus help reduce heat and inflammation. Apply these creams immediately after sun exposure and reapply as needed to promote healing and prevent peeling.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sunburn that are treated with herbal creams are skin irritations such as eczema, minor burns, and abrasions. Herbal creams can provide soothing relief for these conditions by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and calming the skin. Ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula are commonly used in herbal creams to treat these ailments, offering natural and gentle alternatives to traditional treatments.
